data.title: Dai Nippon Printing (TSE:7912) Margin Compression To 5.3% Tests Bullish Narratives
data.description:
Dai Nippon Printing (TSE:7912) has put out a solidly detailed Q3 2026 scorecard, with revenue of ¥389.5 billion and basic EPS of ¥57.16, backed by net income of ¥25.1 billion, giving investors clear line of sight on both the top and bottom line. The company has seen revenue move from ¥370.7 billion in Q3 2025 to ¥389.5 billion in Q3 2026, while quarterly EPS has been in a similar range at ¥57.27 and ¥57.16 respectively. This sets the scene for a results set where the key question is how comfortably margins sit against those recent trends.

See our full analysis for Dai Nippon Printing.

With the latest figures on the table, the next step is to see how these margins and growth signals line up with the widely held narratives around Dai Nippon Printing, and where those stories might need a reset.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

Advertisement

Margins Under Pressure At 5.3%

  • Over the last 12 months, Dai Nippon Printing earned net income of ¥80,001 million on ¥1.5b of revenue, which works out to a 5.3% net profit margin compared with 8.9% in the prior year.
  • What stands out for a bearish take is that this thinner 5.3% margin sits alongside modest forecast growth of about 2.35% a year for earnings and 1.4% a year for revenue, which critics highlight as a sign that profitability and growth are both on the low side.
    • That combination of lower margin and slower forecast revenue growth than the broader JP market forecast of 5% gives bears concrete support for arguing the business is facing pressure on both efficiency and top line.
    • At the same time, trailing earnings are still described as high quality, which challenges the idea that the lower margin is purely about weak underlying performance rather than how costs and mix are flowing through the income statement.

TTM EPS Slips From ¥238.90 To ¥179.17

  • On a trailing 12 month basis, basic EPS moved from ¥238.90 at Q4 2025 to ¥179.17 at Q3 2026, while trailing net income over that span went from ¥110,682 million to ¥80,001 million even as trailing revenue rose from ¥1.46b to ¥1.51b.
  • Supporters with a more bullish mindset often point to the company’s mix of traditional printing and higher value materials, and this EPS pattern gives them a mixed picture to work with.
    • On one hand, five year earnings growth of about 16.8% a year is stronger than the current 2.35% earnings growth forecast, which can be used to argue that the recent period is not the whole story for long term potential.
    • On the other hand, the drop in trailing EPS alongside higher trailing revenue means anyone leaning bullish has to reconcile the longer term growth record with the more recent period where earnings growth was negative.

P/E Of 17.3x And DCF Gap

  • Shares trade on a trailing P/E of 17.3x at a price of ¥3,182, above the JP Commercial Services industry average of 14.5x, below the peer average of 21.9x, and about 20.3% below a DCF fair value estimate of ¥3,991.18.
  • What is interesting here is how this valuation profile interacts with the mixed growth and margin picture.
    • The stock not being the cheapest in the industry by P/E, yet still sitting roughly 20% under the DCF fair value, suggests investors are weighing the 5.3% margin and modest growth forecasts against that modeled upside.
    • Because revenue over the last year sits at about ¥1.51b and earnings are described as high quality, some investors may focus on whether that combination justifies a P/E premium to the industry even with slower forecast growth than the broader JP market.

data.title: Logistea Q3 EPS Jump To SEK 0.52 Challenges Bearish Earnings Narratives
data.description:
Advertisement

Logistea (OM:LOGI A) FY 2025 earnings snapshot

Logistea (OM:LOGI A) has just put fresh numbers on the table, with Q3 FY 2025 revenue at SEK 280 million and basic EPS of SEK 0.52, alongside trailing twelve month revenue of SEK 1,034 million and EPS of SEK 1.69. Over recent periods, the company has seen revenue move from SEK 239 million in Q3 2024 to SEK 280 million in Q3 2025. Quarterly EPS shifted from SEK 0.04 to SEK 0.52, which raises the question of how durable those boosted margins are for shareholders.

See our full analysis for Logistea.

With the latest results on the record, the next step is to see how these figures line up with the key narratives around Logistea, and where the numbers challenge what investors think they know about the business.

See what the community is saying about Logistea

TTM net margin at 79.4% after SEK 582m one off

  • Over the last 12 months, Logistea reported a 79.4% net profit margin, compared with 15.2% a year earlier, with a SEK 582 million one off gain playing a big role in that jump.
  • Critics highlight that such a large one off item makes it harder to judge ongoing profitability, even though trailing net income reached SEK 821 million on SEK 1,034 million of revenue.
    • This very high margin is used to support the bullish idea that recent refinancing and cost reductions are flowing through to profit, but the SEK 582 million gain means part of the uplift is not from regular rental activity.
    • At the same time, the bearish concern is that if you mentally strip out the one off, the 79.4% margin may overstate how much earnings power is tied to recurring rent and property income.

EPS trend and forecasts pull in different directions

  • TTM basic EPS is 1.69 SEK, up from 0.96 SEK over the prior trailing period, while analysts currently forecast a very small 0.3% annual decline in earnings in the coming years.
  • Consensus narrative notes that expansion through Nordic investments and acquisitions is expected to support earnings over time, yet the slight earnings decline forecast and large TTM one off gain contrast with the more bullish long term story.
    • On one hand, TTM net income of SEK 821 million versus SEK 332 million in the prior TTM window aligns with the bullish view that refinancing and portfolio growth have lifted profitability.
    • On the other hand, the forecast 0.3% annual earnings decline supports the bearish argument that recent EPS of 0.52 SEK in Q3 2025 and 1.69 SEK over 12 months may not be a straightforward guide for the next few years.
Over the last few quarters Logistea has produced EPS of 0.33 SEK, 0.51 SEK and 0.52 SEK, so bulls argue the recent level of earnings is starting to look more consistent and worth studying in detail before leaning too much on forecasts alone. 🐂 Logistea Bull Case

Low 8.8x P/E and SEK 20.33 target vs SEK 14.15 price

  • Logistea trades on an 8.8x trailing P/E, below the Swedish real estate industry at 12.9x and peers at 15.9x, while the current share price of SEK 14.15 sits below the SEK 20.33 analyst target and above the SEK 5.24 DCF fair value estimate.
  • Bears argue that weak interest coverage and the influence of the SEK 582 million one off gain mean the low P/E and higher analyst target should be treated carefully.
    • The 8.8x multiple makes the stock look cheaper than the broader Swedish market on 21.2x and the industry on 12.9x, which fits the bullish angle around relative value, but that metric still rests on earnings affected by non recurring income.
    • At the same time, the interest coverage concern and a DCF fair value of SEK 5.24 sitting well below the SEK 14.15 share price support the bearish view that higher funding costs and more normalised earnings could matter more than the headline P/E.
With analysts seeing room between the SEK 14.15 price and their SEK 20.33 target while debt costs and one off gains complicate the picture, skeptics may want to see how the bear case stacks up against the numbers. 🐻 Logistea Bear Case

data.title: Haypp Group (OM:HAYPP) Margin At 1.6% Tests Bullish Earnings Narratives
data.description:
Haypp Group (OM:HAYPP) has put fresh numbers on the table for FY 2025, with third quarter revenue at SEK 962.6 million and basic EPS of SEK 0.15, rounding out a trailing twelve month picture that features SEK 3.75 billion in revenue and EPS of SEK 1.94. Over recent quarters the company has seen revenue move from SEK 950.1 million in Q2 2024 to SEK 951.6 million in Q3 2024, then to SEK 925.9 million in Q4 2024, before landing at SEK 933.3 million, SEK 931.6 million and SEK 962.6 million across Q1, Q2 and Q3 2025. Quarterly EPS shifted from roughly breakeven in Q2 2024 to SEK 0.57, SEK 0.51, SEK 1.01, SEK 0.28 and SEK 0.15 over the same stretch. With earnings over the last year described as high quality and net profit margins moving up to 1.6%, this set of results keeps the focus firmly on how much of that margin trajectory can be sustained.

See our full analysis for Haypp Group.

TTM earnings up 118.4% while quarterly EPS cools

  • Over the last 12 months, earnings growth of 118.4% and trailing EPS of 1.94 SEK sit alongside quarterly EPS stepping down from 1.01 SEK in Q1 2025 to 0.28 SEK in Q2 and 0.15 SEK in Q3.
  • What stands out for the bullish view is that strong trailing growth is being used to justify expectations of about 35.5% annual earnings growth, yet the recent quarterly net income figures of 30.4 million SEK, 8.6 million SEK and 4.5 million SEK for Q1, Q2 and Q3 2025 show how sensitive those hopes are to short term swings.
    • Bulls point to the 118.4% trailing earnings growth and a 5 year average of 73.9% a year as evidence that earnings are the main driver, while the step down from 30.4 million SEK to 4.5 million SEK across 2025 so far is a reminder that quarterly momentum can be choppy.

Strong trailing growth with softer recent quarters is exactly what has bulls and skeptics debating Haypp's next chapter, so it can be useful to see how those numbers are stitched together in the full bull case for the stock. 🐂 Haypp Group Bull Case

Net margin at 1.6% keeps profitability tight

  • Haypp reports a trailing net profit margin of 1.6%, up from 0.7% the year before, on 3.75b SEK of revenue and 58.7 million SEK of net income.
  • Skeptics focus on how thin that 1.6% margin is and argue that even with analysts expecting margins to move toward 4.5% over time, the modest level today means any extra costs or tax changes could quickly eat into earnings.
    • The bearish narrative flags higher spending on U.S. expansion and regulatory compliance as possible headwinds, which could weigh on margins that are currently only a little above 1%.
    • At the same time, the move from 0.7% to 1.6% over the last year gives some support to bears' own assumption that margins can rise, while still leaving plenty of room for their concern that extra investment could cap how far net margins actually go.

  • Consensus narrative supporters point out that this mix of a higher than average P/E and a DCF fair value above both the share price and the 151.67 SEK target reflects how much weight the market is putting on future earnings and margin expansion, not just the current 1.6% net margin.
    • The higher P/E compared with the 22.7x industry average lines up with expectations for around 14% annual revenue growth and meaningful earnings growth, so any change in those forecasts could change how that multiple looks.
    • At the same time, the gap between 114.00 SEK and the 256.63 SEK DCF fair value shows how differently earnings power is being valued when you compare trailing numbers with longer term growth models.

data.title: Elecom (TSE:6750) One Off Gain Drives EPS Surge And Tests Bearish Earnings Decline Narrative
data.description:
Elecom (TSE:6750) has just posted Q3 2026 results with revenue of ¥36,437 million and basic EPS of ¥142.51, while trailing twelve month revenue sits at ¥125.9 billion and EPS at ¥240.20. The company has seen quarterly revenue move from ¥30,196 million in Q2 2026 to ¥36,437 million in Q3 2026, with EPS shifting from ¥31.45 and ¥24.65 in Q2 and Q1 2026 respectively to ¥142.51 in the latest quarter. This sets up a story where higher margins and a sizeable one off gain are key to understanding the quality of these earnings.

See our full analysis for Elecom.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to test them against the prevailing narratives around Elecom, examining where the recent margin profile supports those views and where it pushes investors to rethink the story.

¥7.5b one off lifts 14.7% margin story

  • Trailing 12 month net income of ¥18,502 million on revenue of ¥125,941 million works out to a 14.7% net margin, compared with 8.3% in the prior year. That margin includes a ¥7.5b one off gain that had a big influence on the reported profitability.
  • What stands out for a bearish view is that this higher 14.7% margin and the trailing 92.5% earnings growth are partly tied to that ¥7.5b item, while forecasts still call for earnings to decline about 12.1% per year over the next three years. This means
    • the recent margin level may not line up with the forward picture bears focus on, especially if the one off gain does not repeat, and
    • critics point to the projected multi year earnings decline as a key risk even though the latest year on year earnings figure looks very strong on paper.

EPS swings from ¥24.65 to ¥142.51

  • Within 2026, basic EPS moved from ¥24.65 in Q1 to ¥31.45 in Q2 and then to ¥142.51 in Q3, alongside net income rising from ¥1,882 million and ¥2,402 million in the first two quarters to ¥11,240 million in Q3, showing how much of the trailing EPS of ¥240.20 is concentrated in this latest period.
  • Supporters who take a more optimistic angle might point out that this stronger Q3 sits on top of trailing EPS of ¥240.20, which is higher than the ¥119.23 to ¥133.23 range seen over the prior trailing quarters. Yet
    • the presence of the ¥7.5b one off gain means part of that jump is not from the core business, and
    • the pattern of earlier quarters, where EPS stayed in the ¥24 to ¥39 range, gives a very different picture from the latest headline figure on its own.

P/E 6.9x vs DCF value ¥2,773.90

This is set against a DCF fair value of roughly ¥2,773.90 plus analyst price targets around ¥2,050.00 that together indicate the current price sits below several reference points.
  • For a value oriented bullish argument, the tension is that the share price of ¥1,661 is below both the DCF fair value of ¥2,773.90 and the ¥2,050.00 analyst target, and analysts in the dataset see around 23.4% implied upside. Yet
    • the same analysis also highlights earnings expected to decline about 12.1% per year over the next three years, and

data.title: Eltel (OM:ELTEL) Returns To Profit In Q3 EPS Beat Tests Bearish Margin Narratives
data.description:
Eltel (OM:ELTEL) has just posted third quarter FY 2025 numbers with revenue of €208.2 million and basic EPS of €0.017, setting the tone for a year where the company has moved from loss-making quarters earlier in FY 2025 to a small profit on a trailing twelve month EPS of roughly €0.001. Over recent periods, revenue has ranged from €169.6 million to €226.1 million per quarter while basic EPS has swung between a loss of about €0.035 and a profit of €0.019, giving investors a clear view of how volatile earnings have been as margins stabilise.

See our full analysis for Eltel.

With the headline results on the table, the next step is to see how these margin shifts line up with the most widely held narratives around Eltel and where those stories might need updating.

See what the community is saying about Eltel

Advertisement

Earnings swing from €33.1m loss to roughly breakeven

  • On a trailing 12 month view, net income has moved from a €33.1 million loss in Q4 2024 to a small €0.1 million profit by Q3 2025, with Basic EPS shifting from a loss of €0.211182 to a slight profit of €0.000638 over the same window.
  • Analysts' consensus view sees this recent return to profit as part of a longer earnings rebuild. However, past five year earnings performance averaging a 32.8% annual decline and the influence of a €2.8 million one off gain on the last 12 months mean investors still need to question how durable this profitability really is.
    • The consensus narrative links operational improvement and a broader customer base to steadier margins, while the historical losses in the trailing data keep the risk of setbacks in focus.
    • Forecast earnings growth of about 27.7% per year is set against this mixed track record, so the small current profit does not fully settle the debate on how smooth that path could be.

€2.8m one off skews margin picture

  • The last 12 months include a €2.8 million non recurring gain that helped lift net income to €0.1 million on €804.9 million of revenue, so part of the margin improvement in the trailing figures comes from a single item rather than the core run rate.
  • Bears argue that reliance on large, project based work and early stage turnarounds in Norway, Denmark and Germany could struggle to absorb such one off effects, and the presence of this €2.8 million gain in a period where several quarters still showed losses gives that concern some backing.
    • For example, quarterly net income swung from a €3.6 million loss in Q1 2025 to a €2.6 million profit in Q3 2025, so separating underlying project profitability from one time items is key to testing the bearish view.
    • The bearish narrative also points to contract repricing and execution risk on larger power and data center projects, which would matter more if future periods do not benefit from similar non recurring gains.
On a period where earnings depend partly on a €2.8 million gain and uneven country performance, skeptics see plenty to question in the long term profit story. 🐻 Eltel Bear Case

Low 0.2x P/S and DCF fair value of €19.10 vs price

  • Eltel is trading on a P/S of 0.2x compared with 0.6x for the wider European Construction industry and 0.3x for peers, and an estimated DCF fair value of €19.10 sits well above the current share price of €9.24, implying the stock trades at around half of that modelled value.
  • Supporters highlight that forecast revenue growth of roughly 2.6% per year and earnings growth of about 27.7% per year, combined with nine consecutive quarters of profitability improvements in the consensus narrative, line up with these valuation gaps, while critics point to weak interest coverage as a reason the discount could persist.
    • On the supportive side, consensus assumes margins can rise from about 0.0% today to 1.8% in three years. If achieved, this would fit with a higher earnings base against the current low P/S and the €19.10 DCF fair value.
    • On the cautious side, the risk summary flags that interest payments are not well covered by earnings, so even with an analyst price target of €9.90 slightly above the €9.24 share price, balance sheet strain remains embedded in the story.
Supporters and skeptics are reading the same low P/S and DCF gap very differently, which is exactly what our community narratives unpack in more detail for this earnings window. 📊 Read the full Eltel Consensus Narrative.

data.title: Assessing FirstCash Holdings (FCFS) Valuation After Strong Recent Share Price Momentum
data.description:
Advertisement

Why FirstCash Holdings Is On Investors’ Radar

FirstCash Holdings (FCFS) has been drawing attention after a solid past 3 months for the stock, prompting investors to revisit how its pawn and retail POS payment operations line up with recent performance.

See our latest analysis for FirstCash Holdings.

The recent 8.3% 1 month share price return, 13.7% 3 month share price return and 16.0% year to date share price return sit alongside a 1 year total shareholder return of 56.5%, suggesting that momentum has been building over time.

If this move in FirstCash has you thinking about where else capital is flowing, it could be worth scanning 23 top founder-led companies as a way to spot other potential long term compounders.

With FirstCash trading at US$181.89 and carrying a value score of 1 plus an analyst price target of US$201.40, the key question is whether the recent momentum still leaves upside on the table or if the market is already pricing in future growth.

Price-to-Earnings of 24.2x: Is It Justified?

At a last close of $181.89, FirstCash Holdings is trading on a P/E of 24.2x, which screens as expensive against several benchmarks and raises questions about how much future profit growth is already reflected in the price.

The P/E multiple compares the current share price to earnings per share, so you are effectively seeing how many dollars investors are willing to pay for each dollar of earnings. For a company like FirstCash, with pawn operations and retail POS payment solutions, this ratio gives a quick feel for how the market is weighing its earnings track record and forecast profit growth.

On one side, FirstCash is described as having high quality earnings, with earnings forecast to grow 16.66% per year and revenue forecast to grow 9.8% per year. Earnings growth over the past 5 years is reported at 20.1% per year, and earnings growth over the past year of 27.6% sits above that 5 year average. That kind of profile can support a higher P/E if investors expect the trend in profitability to continue.

On the other side, several reference points suggest the current P/E could be demanding. First, compared to the estimated fair P/E of 15.9x, the current 24.2x is higher, which implies the market price is rich relative to what the SWS fair ratio model suggests the multiple could move toward. Second, against the US Consumer Finance industry average P/E of 8x, FirstCash trades at a much higher level, pointing to a premium that assumes stronger or more resilient earnings than the typical peer. The shares are also trading above the SWS DCF model estimate of future cash flow value of $83.26, which is another sign that the current price bakes in optimistic expectations.

Put simply, the market is assigning FirstCash a P/E multiple that is well above both the sector average and the estimated fair multiple. Anyone looking at the stock needs to decide whether the company’s growth profile and earnings quality are enough to support that premium or whether the valuation could eventually settle closer to the fair ratio level suggested by the model.

data.title: Is Sphere Entertainment (SPHR) Pricing Reflect Lofty Expectations After Las Vegas Sphere Buzz?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Sphere Entertainment's share price matches its underlying worth, you are not alone. This article is designed to help you frame that question clearly.
  • The stock last closed at US$115.70, with returns of 24.1% over 7 days, 17.8% over 30 days, 22.7% year to date and 140.0% over 1 year. These figures naturally raise questions about how much future upside or downside is already reflected in the price.
  • Recent coverage has focused on Sphere Entertainment's high profile Sphere venue in Las Vegas and broader interest in live entertainment experiences. This has kept the company on many investors' radars. This backdrop helps explain why some market participants are rethinking the balance between potential growth and the risks tied to a single flagship asset.
  • Our valuation model gives Sphere Entertainment a valuation score of 2 out of 6, which reflects the number of checks where the stock screens as undervalued. Next, we will walk through the different approaches behind that score and touch on a more holistic way to think about value at the end of the article.

Sphere Entertainment scores just 2/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Sphere Entertainment Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company could be worth today by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting those back to a present value.

For Sphere Entertainment, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flow projections in US$. The latest twelve month free cash flow is a loss of $272.47 million, so the valuation leans heavily on future expectations rather than current cash generation.

Analyst and extrapolated estimates suggest free cash flow moving into positive territory over the coming years, reaching $469.00 million by 2030. Intermediate projections include $151.80 million in 2026 and $408.00 million in 2029, with later years extrapolated by Simply Wall St rather than directly forecast by analysts.

When all projected cash flows are discounted back using this 2 stage model, the estimated intrinsic value comes out at about $201.05 per share. Compared to the recent share price of $115.70, this implies the stock screens as about 42.5% undervalued on this DCF view.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Sphere Entertainment is undervalued by 42.5%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 53 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Sphere Entertainment.

Approach 2: Sphere Entertainment Price vs Earnings (P/E)

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful way to see how much investors are paying for each dollar of earnings, because it ties the share price directly to the underlying profit figure.

A higher or lower P/E can often be linked to what the market expects for future earnings growth and how risky those earnings appear. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can support a higher P/E, while lower growth or higher risk tends to line up with a lower, more cautious multiple.

Sphere Entertainment currently trades on a P/E of 122.94x, compared with an Entertainment industry average of 32.74x and a peer group average of 43.93x. Simply Wall St also calculates a proprietary “Fair Ratio” of 5.82x. This is the P/E it would expect given factors such as the company’s earnings profile, industry, profit margins, market cap and specific risks.

This Fair Ratio aims to be more tailored than a simple peer or industry comparison, because it attempts to adjust for company specific growth, risk and profitability rather than assuming one size fits all.

When set against the Fair Ratio of 5.82x, the current P/E of 122.94x suggests Sphere Entertainment screens as expensive on this earnings based view.

Result: OVERVALUED

data.title: SMC Corporation Just Beat EPS By 19%: Here's What Analysts Think Will Happen Next
data.description:
It's been a good week for SMC Corporation (TSE:6273) shareholders, because the company has just released its latest quarterly results, and the shares gained 9.5% to JP¥70,840. It looks like a credible result overall - although revenues of JP¥210b were in line with what the analysts predicted, SMC surprised by delivering a statutory profit of JP¥671 per share, a notable 19% above expectations. The analysts typically update their forecasts at each earnings report, and we can judge from their estimates whether their view of the company has changed or if there are any new concerns to be aware of. With this in mind, we've gathered the latest statutory forecasts to see what the analysts are expecting for next year.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Taking into account the latest results, the consensus forecast from SMC's 17 analysts is for revenues of JP¥895.4b in 2027. This reflects a notable 10% improvement in revenue compared to the last 12 months. Per-share earnings are expected to rise 5.6% to JP¥2,636. In the lead-up to this report, the analysts had been modelling revenues of JP¥889.5b and earnings per share (EPS) of JP¥2,612 in 2027. So it's pretty clear that, although the analysts have updated their estimates, there's been no major change in expectations for the business following the latest results.

View our latest analysis for SMC

The consensus price target rose 8.4% to JP¥66,450despite there being no meaningful change to earnings estimates. It could be that the analystsare reflecting the predictability of SMC's earnings by assigning a price premium. That's not the only conclusion we can draw from this data however, as some investors also like to consider the spread in estimates when evaluating analyst price targets. Currently, the most bullish analyst values SMC at JP¥100,000 per share, while the most bearish prices it at JP¥48,000. This is a fairly broad spread of estimates, suggesting that analysts are forecasting a wide range of possible outcomes for the business.

Of course, another way to look at these forecasts is to place them into context against the industry itself. The analysts are definitely expecting SMC's growth to accelerate, with the forecast 8.2% annualised growth to the end of 2027 ranking favourably alongside historical growth of 5.7% per annum over the past five years. By contrast, our data suggests that other companies (with analyst coverage) in a similar industry are forecast to grow their revenue at 5.1% per year. Factoring in the forecast acceleration in revenue, it's pretty clear that SMC is expected to grow much faster than its industry.

Advertisement

The Bottom Line

The most obvious conclusion is that there's been no major change in the business' prospects in recent times, with the analysts holding their earnings forecasts steady, in line with previous estimates. Fortunately, they also reconfirmed their revenue numbers, suggesting that it's tracking in line with expectations. Additionally, our data suggests that revenue is expected to grow faster than the wider industry. There was also a nice increase in the price target, with the analysts clearly feeling that the intrinsic value of the business is improving.

Following on from that line of thought, we think that the long-term prospects of the business are much more relevant than next year's earnings. We have estimates - from multiple SMC analysts - going out to 2028, and you can see them free on our platform here.

Don't forget that there may still be risks. For instance, we've identified 1 warning sign for SMC that you should be aware of.

data.title: Kajima Corporation Just Beat Analyst Forecasts, And Analysts Have Been Updating Their Predictions
data.description:
A week ago, Kajima Corporation (TSE:1812) came out with a strong set of quarterly numbers that could potentially lead to a re-rate of the stock. The company beat forecasts, with revenue of JP¥773b, some 2.8% above estimates, and statutory earnings per share (EPS) coming in at JP¥96.29, 31% ahead of expectations. Following the result, the analysts have updated their earnings model, and it would be good to know whether they think there's been a strong change in the company's prospects, or if it's business as usual. We've gathered the most recent statutory forecasts to see whether the analysts have changed their earnings models, following these results.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Taking into account the latest results, Kajima's seven analysts currently expect revenues in 2027 to be JP¥3.03t, approximately in line with the last 12 months. Statutory earnings per share are expected to shrink 3.4% to JP¥360 in the same period. In the lead-up to this report, the analysts had been modelling revenues of JP¥3.04t and earnings per share (EPS) of JP¥363 in 2027. So it's pretty clear that, although the analysts have updated their estimates, there's been no major change in expectations for the business following the latest results.

See our latest analysis for Kajima

The consensus price target rose 5.0% to JP¥6,593despite there being no meaningful change to earnings estimates. It could be that the analystsare reflecting the predictability of Kajima's earnings by assigning a price premium. Fixating on a single price target can be unwise though, since the consensus target is effectively the average of analyst price targets. As a result, some investors like to look at the range of estimates to see if there are any diverging opinions on the company's valuation. The most optimistic Kajima analyst has a price target of JP¥9,900 per share, while the most pessimistic values it at JP¥3,300. As you can see the range of estimates is wide, with the lowest valuation coming in at less than half the most bullish estimate, suggesting there are some strongly diverging views on how analysts think this business will perform. As a result it might not be a great idea to make decisions based on the consensus price target, which is after all just an average of this wide range of estimates.

Another way we can view these estimates is in the context of the bigger picture, such as how the forecasts stack up against past performance, and whether forecasts are more or less bullish relative to other companies in the industry. We would highlight that Kajima's revenue growth is expected to slow, with the forecast 0.09% annualised growth rate until the end of 2027 being well below the historical 9.9% p.a. growth over the last five years. Compare this against other companies (with analyst forecasts) in the industry, which are in aggregate expected to see revenue growth of 3.5% annually. Factoring in the forecast slowdown in growth, it seems obvious that Kajima is also expected to grow slower than other industry participants.

Advertisement

The Bottom Line

The most important thing to take away is that there's been no major change in sentiment, with the analysts reconfirming that the business is performing in line with their previous earnings per share estimates. On the plus side, there were no major changes to revenue estimates; although forecasts imply they will perform worse than the wider industry. We note an upgrade to the price target, suggesting that the analysts believes the intrinsic value of the business is likely to improve over time.

With that in mind, we wouldn't be too quick to come to a conclusion on Kajima. Long-term earnings power is much more important than next year's profits. We have forecasts for Kajima going out to 2028, and you can see them free on our platform here.

For investors watching Alamos Gold at CA$61.61, the Island Gold study adds fresh context to a share price that has moved up 12.3% over the past week and 16.9% year to date. The stock is also up 95.8% over the past year and has delivered a very large gain over five years, which puts extra attention on how this expansion plan might influence future operations.

The expansion study focuses on lifting gold production and using the Magino mill as a larger processing hub for the district. Ongoing exploration, along with the 30% reserve increase, gives investors more detail on how Alamos Gold is planning around longer term mine life and potential output from Island Gold.

The Island Gold District Expansion Study points to Alamos Gold leaning harder into a hub-and-spoke model around the Magino mill, with high grade ore from Island Gold and nearby deposits feeding a larger processing center. For you as an investor, the 30% reserve uplift and planned higher throughput tie directly into the company’s multi year production guidance, which outlines a path from 570,000 to 650,000 ounces in 2026 to as much as 875,000 ounces in 2028. This scale aims to put Alamos in the same conversation as larger Canadian producers such as Agnico Eagle and Yamana’s successor entities, while still competing with mid tier names such as B2Gold on growth through organic projects.

How This Fits Into The Alamos Gold Narrative

The Excess Returns model looks at how much profit a company is expected to generate above the return that equity investors require, then capitalizes those surplus profits into an intrinsic value per share.

data.description:
• In February 2026, IREN Limited reported second-quarter 2025 results showing revenue rising to US$184.69 million while net loss widened to US$155.41 million, alongside six‑month revenue of US$424.99 million and net income of US$229.20 million compared with a loss a year earlier.
  • Ahead of its inclusion in the MSCI USA Index later in February, IREN’s shift from Bitcoin mining toward AI infrastructure, anchored by a US$9.70 billion Microsoft contract and large new data center capacity, is reshaping how investors assess its business mix and risk profile.
  • Next, we’ll examine how IREN’s upcoming MSCI USA Index inclusion and Microsoft-backed AI expansion affect its existing investment narrative.

IREN Investment Narrative Recap

To own IREN today, you have to believe its pivot from Bitcoin mining toward AI infrastructure can offset earnings volatility and capital intensity. The latest results highlight that tension: strong six month revenue and profit, but a sharply wider Q2 net loss, while the Microsoft AI contract and February MSCI USA Index inclusion remain the key near term catalyst. The biggest risk is execution and financing around massive data center and GPU build outs; this news does not remove that concern.

The upcoming inclusion in the MSCI USA Index after 27 February 2026 looks most relevant here, as it could pull more institutional and passive capital into the stock just as IREN absorbs higher losses tied to its expansion. That added visibility sits alongside the US$9.70 billion Microsoft agreement and new Oklahoma and Texas capacity, which together shape how quickly the AI cloud story can begin to matter more than quarterly Bitcoin and capex swings.
